questions.q: How many separate terms did Atal Bihari Vajpayee serve as Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Vajpayee served three separate terms as Prime Minister of India.

questions.q: Vajpayee served a full term as Prime Minister during which years?
questions.explanation: His full term as Prime Minister ran from 1999 to 2004.

questions.q: Vajpayee's birthday, 25 December, is observed in India as which day?
questions.explanation: His birthday, 25 December, is observed as Good Governance Day.

questions.q: Vajpayee's deck subtitle, 'The Orator Statesman,' highlights his renown for which skill?
questions.explanation: He was widely renowned for his oratory, reflected in the 'Orator Statesman' subtitle.

questions.q: Vajpayee is noted as the first Prime Minister from outside which party to complete a full term in office?
questions.explanation: He was the first non-Congress Prime Minister to complete a full term in office.

questions.q: Vajpayee led which coalition during his full term as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: His 1999-2004 government was led by the National Democratic Alliance (NDA).

questions.q: Whom did Vajpayee succeed as Prime Minister when he formed his second government in March 1998?
questions.explanation: Vajpayee succeeded I.K. Gujral as Prime Minister in March 1998 after that year's general election.

questions.q: Vajpayee's full term as Prime Minister ended in which year?
questions.explanation: His full term ended in 2004.

questions.q: In which month is Good Governance Day, marking Vajpayee's birthday, observed each year?
questions.explanation: Good Governance Day is observed on 25 December, in December.

questions.q: As Prime Minister, Vajpayee was the head of which branch of the Union government?
questions.explanation: As Prime Minister he headed the executive branch of the Union government.

questions.q: Vajpayee's first term as Prime Minister in 1996 lasted approximately how long?
questions.explanation: His first term in 1996 lasted about thirteen days before he resigned ahead of a confidence vote.

questions.q: Vajpayee's second term as Prime Minister, beginning in 1998, lasted roughly how long before fresh elections?
questions.explanation: His second term, from 1998, lasted about thirteen months before the government lost a confidence vote.

questions.q: During his premiership, Vajpayee was a member of which House of Parliament?
questions.explanation: Vajpayee sat in the Lok Sabha as Prime Minister, representing his constituency directly.

questions.q: Which Lok Sabha constituency did Vajpayee represent for several terms during his career?
questions.explanation: Vajpayee represented the Lucknow constituency in the Lok Sabha for several terms.

questions.q: Who succeeded Vajpayee as Prime Minister of India in 2004?
questions.explanation: Manmohan Singh became Prime Minister in 2004, succeeding Vajpayee.

questions.q: Before becoming Prime Minister, Vajpayee held which Union cabinet portfolio in the Janata Party government of the late 1970s?
questions.explanation: Vajpayee served as Minister of External Affairs in the Janata Party government from 1977 to 1979.

questions.q: Vajpayee began his third term and full tenure as Prime Minister following the general election to which Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: His 1999-2004 full term followed the election that constituted the 13th Lok Sabha.

questions.q: Which national highway development programme was launched during Vajpayee's tenure as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: The Golden Quadrilateral highway project was launched during Vajpayee's premiership.

questions.q: Good Governance Day was first observed as an annual occasion on Vajpayee's birthday beginning in which year?
questions.explanation: Good Governance Day was instituted on 25 December and first observed in 2014.

questions.q: Vajpayee co-founded which political party that later led the National Democratic Alliance he headed as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Vajpayee was a founder-leader of the Bharatiya Janata Party, which led the NDA government he headed.

questions.q: India's highest civilian honour was conferred on Vajpayee. Which award is that?
questions.explanation: Vajpayee was conferred the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian honour.

questions.q: During Vajpayee's tenure, three new states were carved out in the year 2000. Which of the following was one of them?
questions.explanation: Uttarakhand, along with Jharkhand and Chhattisgarh, was created in 2000 during Vajpayee's tenure.

questions.q: On which date in 2004 did Vajpayee's full term as Prime Minister come to an end?
questions.explanation: Vajpayee tendered his resignation and his successor took office on 22 May 2004.

questions.q: Vajpayee's brief first term as Prime Minister began on which date in 1996?
questions.explanation: His first, short-lived term began on 16 May 1996.

questions.q: Vajpayee began his second term as Prime Minister in which month of 1998?
questions.explanation: He began his second term in March 1998 after that year's general election.

questions.q: Which 2001 diplomatic summit with Pakistan was hosted during Vajpayee's tenure as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: The Agra Summit between India and Pakistan was held in 2001 during Vajpayee's premiership.

questions.q: Vajpayee's parliamentary career in the Lok Sabha began with his first election to that House in which decade?
questions.explanation: Vajpayee was first elected to the Lok Sabha in 1957, in the 1950s.

questions.q: Vajpayee's second government, formed in 1998, lost a confidence vote in 1999 by what margin in the Lok Sabha?
options: It was not put to a vote
questions.explanation: His 1998 government lost the 1999 confidence vote by a single vote, leading to fresh elections.

questions.q: The Bharat Ratna was conferred on Vajpayee in which year?
questions.explanation: Vajpayee was conferred the Bharat Ratna in 2014.

questions.q: As Prime Minister, Vajpayee was appointed to the office under which constitutional article governing the appointment of the Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is appointed by the President under Article 75, the provision under which Vajpayee took office.
